description: A required quantum gate is absent from the circuit
An unintended quantum gate is added to the circuit
A gate is replaced with an incorrect gate
A gate is applied to the wrong qubit or qubits
Qubits are ordered or mapped incorrectly
The circuit assumes or initializes the wrong number of qubits
A required measurement operation is missing
A measurement is inserted too early or unnecessarily
A qubit is measured repeatedly or too many measurements are performed
A quantum operation is incorrectly applied after measurement
An inverse or cleanup operation is applied incorrectly and leaves invalid state
A rotation angle or parameter value is incorrect
A gate parameter has the wrong sign
A parameter is assigned to the wrong gate or position
A correct gate is placed in the wrong position
A control qubit is added removed or assigned incorrectly
A custom gate is invalid or not recognized by the framework
The number of classical bits does not match the number of measured qubits
A circuit or internal representation drops or stores incorrect information
The program uses the wrong bit ordering or endian convention
Quantum operations are scheduled at incorrect times
A formula probability amplitude or matrix computation is incorrect
Random seed sampling or probability behavior is handled incorrectly
The program uses the quantum framework API incorrectly
A program works on one backend or simulator but fails on another
